commit.message:
Fix game hang by correcting CPU branch timing

Branch taken (+1) and page-crossing (+2) cycle penalties were lost because
branch() incremented this.cycles but execute() overwrote it with the base
cost. Use a branchPenalty field that gets added after execute() returns.
Also remove temporary debug instrumentation from PPU and NesEmulator.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add component diagram in draw.io format

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix game hang after screensaver by delivering APU frame counter IRQs to CPU

The APU's isIrqPending() was never checked in the main loop, so games
relying on APU IRQs for timing (e.g. Double Dragon & Battletoads) would
spin indefinitely waiting for an interrupt that never fired.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add APU implementation for sound support

Implement NES Audio Processing Unit with pulse, triangle, and noise
channels, wired into the CPU bus and main emulation loop. Audio output
uses javax.sound.sampled at 44100 Hz.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix game freeze after title screen by adding NMI edge detection, PRG RAM, and DMA NMI handling

- Trigger NMI immediately when game enables NMI via PPUCTRL while VBlank is active
- Add 8KB PRG RAM at $6000-$7FFF so game state reads/writes work correctly
- Check for NMI during DMA transfer to avoid missing VBlank interrupts

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(ui): use CSS variables directly in SVG to fix stale colours on theme toggle

getCssVar() read CSS variables once at React render time. When the user
switched between Light/Dark mode, the BeamSystemSketch component did not
re-render (no store/prop change), leaving SVG elements with stale colours
(e.g. support labels A/B in near-black #0a0a0a on a dark background).

Fix: replace getCssVar calls with literal CSS variable strings
("var(--foreground)" etc.). The browser resolves these at paint time,
always using the current theme – no React re-render needed.

commit.message:
Sort All Transactions by createdAt instead of mixed date field (#52)

Previously, unifiedTransactions in money/page.tsx and dashboard/page.tsx
were sorted by the `date` field, which was populated from different sources:
fine.date (event date), payment.date (payment date), and duePayment.createdAt
(creation timestamp). This caused inconsistent ordering across types.

Add a `createdAt` field to UnifiedTransaction and sort by it:
- Fine: fine.createdAt (required on all fines)
- Payment: payment.createdAt || payment.date (optional, fall back to date)
- Due Payment: duePayment.createdAt

Also removes the unused _due variable in money/page.tsx.

Closes #41

Co-authored-by: Heiko Rademacher <rademacher.heiko@gmail.com>

commit.message:
Move sandbox data from virtiofs to virtio-blk for snapshot/restore

virtiofs can't preserve FUSE file handle state across VM snapshot/restore,
causing "Bad file descriptor" errors. virtio-blk disk images are managed
entirely by cloud-hypervisor and snapshot correctly.

- Mount data.img at /opt/choiros/data/sandbox (was /var/lib/choiros)
- Remove choiros-data virtiofs share (3 shares remain: nix-store, bin, creds)
- Update runtime-ctl socket wait count (4 → 3)
- Simplify data snapshots to copy data.img file (reflink on btrfs)

commit.message:
fix(tui): use full results-strip width for pane scroll capacity

visible_pane_capacity() was using the active pane's inner rect width
(a single pane) instead of the full results-strip width, causing it
to compute capacity=1 in multi-pane mode. When the detail pane was
open, this mismatch between navigation-time capacity (too small) and
render-time capacity (correct) meant the scroll offset wouldn't
advance far enough to reveal off-screen panes during navigation.

Fix: store the full results-strip inner rect before the pane loop
instead of storing individual pane inner rects per-pane. Also add
adjust_pane_scroll_offset() after directional focus navigation.

Fixes #107

fix: use typo-corrected query for SQL and semantic search

The typo correction in analyzer.ts was working (e.g., "fud" → "food")
but the corrected text was only used for cache keys and summaries.
The actual SQL and semantic search queries used `analysis.raw` (the
original uncorrected query), so typo-corrected queries returned zero
results.

Added `corrected` field to QueryAnalysis and updated comprehensive
search strategy to use it for all search operations. Demographic
detection and exclusion filters still use `raw` (original intent).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Secure ElevenLabs API key with server-side proxy (#2)

* fix(tts): resolve voice-ID mismatch, pending promise, and client-side key exposure

- SettingsPage: replace OpenAI-style voice names (alloy/echo/…) with real
  ElevenLabs pre-made voice IDs (Rachel/Josh/Dorothy/Bella/Elli). Default
  changed from "alloy" to Rachel (21m00Tcm4TlvDq8ikWAM).

- tts.ts: add module-level `resolveActive` callback so `stopSpeaking()`
  immediately resolves the Promise returned by `speakText()` instead of
  leaving it pending forever.

- tts.ts + vite.config.ts: route TTS calls through /api/tts proxy so the
  ElevenLabs API key is injected server-side and never bundled into the
  client. The Vite dev-server proxy reads ELEVENLABS_API_KEY (no VITE_
  prefix) and adds the xi-api-key header before forwarding to ElevenLabs.

- env.ts: remove apiKey from the client-visible ELEVENLABS config object;
  update the default voiceId fallback to Rachel.

- .env.example: rename VITE_ELEVENLABS_API_KEY → ELEVENLABS_API_KEY with
  an explanation of why it must stay server-side; document all five
  pre-made voice IDs; update default VITE_ELEVENLABS_VOICE_ID to Rachel.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01TDrt4ie5Tdkxv8diea5YTR

* fix(tts): add production server, vite preview proxy, and localStorage migration

Production server (no-backend static build fix):
- Add server.cjs: minimal Node.js HTTP server (built-ins only) that serves
  dist/ and proxies POST /api/tts/:voiceId to ElevenLabs, injecting
  xi-api-key server-side. Run with: ELEVENLABS_API_KEY=<k> node server.cjs
- Add package.json "start" script (node server.cjs) and "preview" script
- Refactor vite.config.ts: replace server.proxy with a ttsProxyPlugin that
  attaches the same middleware to both configureServer (vite dev) and
  configurePreviewServer (vite preview), closing the gap where `vite preview`
  would 404 /api/tts even though the key was available.

Legacy localStorage migration (voice-ID validation):
- Add ELEVENLABS_VOICES (canonical voice list) and resolveStoredVoiceId()
  to tts.ts. The resolver validates the stored value against the known set of
  ElevenLabs voice IDs and falls back to Rachel (21m00Tcm4TlvDq8ikWAM) when
  it finds a legacy name like "alloy". It also writes the corrected ID back to
  localStorage so a single page-load cleans up old installs.
- SettingsPage: import ELEVENLABS_VOICES (single source of truth for voice
  list) and use resolveStoredVoiceId as the useState initialiser.
- HomePage: replace both raw localStorage.getItem("voice-selectedVoice")
  reads with resolveStoredVoiceId() so legacy IDs never reach speakText().

https://claude.ai/code/session_01TDrt4ie5Tdkxv8diea5YTR

* fix(tts): abort in-flight fetch when stopSpeaking() is called

Previously stopSpeaking() only paused the HTMLAudioElement, so a slow
network response could still complete and start audio playback after the
user pressed stop.

Changes:
- Add module-level `activeController: AbortController | null` that is
  created at the top of each speakText() call and stored before the fetch.
- Pass `controller.signal` to fetch() so the browser cancels the request
  immediately when abort() is called.
- stopSpeaking() now calls activeController.abort() before touching the
  audio element, guaranteeing no response body is consumed after stop.
- AbortError is caught and returned silently (it is expected, not a bug).

https://claude.ai/code/session_01TDrt4ie5Tdkxv8diea5YTR

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: tag filtering and integration test failures (#199) (#202)

* fix: handle list-type tags in _filter_tasks_by_tags (#199)

AppleScript returns tags as JSON arrays, but _filter_tasks_by_tags()
assumed comma-separated strings and called .split(',') on the list,
causing AttributeError. Now handles both list and string formats.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: add ensure_test_tags fixture for tag integration tests (#199)

Integration tests using tags (test-work, test-urgent, urgent, work) failed
because those tags didn't exist in the test database. Added ensure_test_tags
class-scoped fixture that creates the required tags via AppleScript before
tests run. Uses `front document` to target the active (test) database.

commit.message:
feat: CWD-based preflight context injection

Detect project from working directory at server startup and inject
a compact table-of-contents into MCP server instructions. Three-tier
fuzzy matching (normalized exact, substring with length guard,
SequenceMatcher >= 0.75). Three SQL queries per project: expiring/
recently expired entries, recent decisions+lessons, active conventions.
Pure SQL, no LLM calls, ~500 tokens.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

DACA v0.1.0 — DaVinci Accelerated Compute Architecture

DACA is to Ascend what CUDA is to NVIDIA and ROCm is to AMD.

A compute platform library for Huawei Ascend 910ProA NPUs that:
- Closes operator gaps (SiLU, SwiGLU missing in MindSpore)
- Fixes CANN bugs (bf16 crash, LayerNorm fp16 crash)
- Optimizes performance (FlashAttention, graph mode)
- Enables CUDA-ecosystem code to run on Ascend

Key features:
- bf16 → fp16 transparent shim (hardware doesn't support bf16)
- LayerNorm with fp32 upcast (avoids CANN fusion bug)
- FlashAttention wrapper with BMM fallback
- Rotary embeddings, RMSNorm, SiLU/SwiGLU activations
- Multi-NPU communication via HCCL
- Graph mode with safe environment variables
- MindSpore/MindFormers compatibility patches
- Hardware probe and diagnostics tools

Pure Python. No sudo. No custom kernels. Runs on OpenI VMs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

halves recoil, and minimizes movement penalty on crosshair.

commit.message:
Fix: Save Exchange runs parallel with Output Router, not in series

Postgres INSERT node returns {"success": true} which wiped out Format
Response data (outputMode, text, binary). Output Router couldn't match
any rule and fell through to fallback — no message sent.

Fix: Format Response feeds both Output Router and Save Exchange in
parallel instead of serial chain.

commit.message:
Add continuous model integrity monitoring with pre-inference verification (M12)

Every 15 minutes, a systemd timer verifies SHA256 hashes of all promoted models.
Tampered models are auto-quarantined and workers restarted. All chat endpoints
now verify model integrity before allowing inference, blocking requests if the
active model fails hash verification. Includes security hardening roadmap (M12-M24).
